quoteFirst: David kept the envelope, sipped his milk, and for some time talked with Mrs. Carter about her cows, her fruit, and whether the white calf was to be sold or kept. When it was
quoteLast: by the big parlor clock he rose to go, said that he hoped to see baby next time, if he might call again, and shook hands. But in going out, from force of habit, he glanced at his watch, and now saw that it was really ten minutes past eight.
title: The Late Tenant
author: Tracy, Louis, 1863-1928

quoteFirst: The dinner to which Sam had been bidden that night was at the house of his old friend, Mr. Willoughby Braddock, in John Street, Mayfair, and at
quoteLast: Mr. Braddock was fidgeting about the morning-room, interviewing his housekeeper, Mrs. Martha Lippett. His guests would be arriving at any moment, and for the last quarter of an hour, a-twitter with the nervousness of an anxious host, he had been popping about the place on a series of tours of inspection, as jumpy, to quote the words of Sleddon, his butler--whom, by leaping suddenly out from the dimly lit dining-room, he had caused to bite his tongue and nearly drop a tray of glasses--as an old hen.
title: Sam in the Suburbs
author: Wodehouse, P. G. (Pelham Grenville), 1881-1975

quoteFirst: Reports of submarines being seen were received between 4 P.M. and dark, from the _Galatea_, _Phaeton_, _Bellona_, _Dublin_, _Southampton_, _Lion_, _Royal Sovereign_ (two submarines), _Queen Elizabeth_, and _Inflexible_, the last ship reporting that two torpedoes had been fired at her at
quoteLast: , and that both had passed close astern; at this time the Battle Fleet and Battle Cruiser Fleet were in company.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

title: The Immortals: Masterpieces of Fiction, Crowned by the French Academy — Complete
author: Various

quoteFirst: "I once knew a man who dined at
quoteLast: ," she said, "which struck me as extremely curious. He was an archdeacon. I believe all archdeacons dine at ten minutes to eight. And they call it a quarter to, which is even odder."
title: Mammon and Co.
author: Benson, E. F. (Edward Frederic), 1867-1940

quoteFirst: On the Monday morning after the Beano he was painting an upper room in a house where several other men were working, and it was customary for the coddy to shout “Yo! Ho!” at mealtimes, to let the hands know when it was time to leave off work. At about
quoteLast: , Jim had squared the part of the work he had been doing—the window—so he decided not to start on the door or the skirting until after breakfast. Whilst he was waiting for the foreman to shout “Yo! Ho!” his mind reverted to the Beano, and he began to hum the tunes of some of the songs that had been sung.
title: The Ragged Trousered Philanthropists
author: Tressell, Robert, 1870-1911

quoteFirst: She returned to her room and dressed with feverish haste, trying to gather her wits for an ordeal which she felt it would have killed her to delay. At
quoteLast: she emerged again and glanced anxiously at Mrs. Holt's door; and scarcely had she reached the lower hall before he drove into the circle.
title: A Modern Chronicle — Complete
author: Churchill, Winston, 1871-1947
